industries.name: Radiopharmaceuticals & Cancer Treatment
uses: Radium-223 dichloride (Xofigo, Bayer): FDA approved 2013 for treatment of castration-resistant prostate cancer with bone metastases — the first alpha-emitting radiopharmaceutical approved for cancer treatment. Ra-223 mimics calcium, concentrating in bone metastases and delivering localized alpha radiation with minimal systemic effects

Radium-223 produces a 'daughter therapy' effect: its alpha-emitting decay products (Rn-219, Po-215, Pb-211, Bi-211, Tl-207) also irradiate tumor tissue as they decay in rapid succession before the chain reaches stable lead-207

Radium-224 (²²⁴Ra, t½ = 3.66 days) in diffusing alpha-emitters radiation therapy (DaRT): radium-224 seeds implanted in solid tumors release radon-220 and polonium-216 alpha emitters that diffuse through the tumor — Phase II trials for skin, recurrent head and neck, and pancreatic cancers

Historical brachytherapy: radium-226 was the first radioactive material used for cancer treatment (1901 by Pierre Curie), in sealed 'radium needles' implanted directly in tumors — now completely replaced by ¹⁹²Ir and other modern sources

companies.name: Bayer AG (Algeta acquisition)
companies.use: Sole manufacturer of Xofigo (radium-223 dichloride). Produces Ra-223 by neutron irradiation of Ra-226 targets in research reactors. Xofigo has treated hundreds of thousands of prostate cancer patients since 2013 approval — the only approved radium therapeutic.

companies.name: Alpha Tau Medical
companies.use: Developing DaRT (diffusing alpha-emitters radiation therapy) using radium-224 seeds for local treatment of solid tumors. Its Alpha DaRT technology is in clinical trials for multiple solid tumor types including skin, head and neck, and pancreatic cancers.

industries.name: Industrial & Legacy Applications
uses: Radium-226 (²²⁶Ra, t½ = 1,600 years) was historically used in radioluminescent watch and instrument dials ('Radium Girls' era, 1910s–1960s) — now entirely replaced by tritium and promethium luminescent paints. Legacy radium-dial contamination remains an environmental remediation challenge

Radium-beryllium (Ra-Be) neutron sources: ²²⁶Ra alpha particles on beryllium produce neutrons — now replaced by ²⁴¹Am-Be, ²⁵²Cf, or electronic neutron generators in all new applications due to Ra-226's long half-life and gamma radiation hazard

Radium sulfate and barium-radium sulfate scale in oil and gas production: radium co-precipitates with barium sulfate scale in produced water, creating NORM (naturally occurring radioactive material) waste requiring specialized disposal at offshore platforms and onshore facilities
Radium research: Ra-226 used as parent source for generating Rn-222 for research, and Ra-228 for nuclear structure studies

companies.name: Clean Harbors
companies.share: ~20% of US radioactive waste disposal market
companies.use: Major radioactive and hazardous waste management company. Handles legacy radium-contaminated materials from watch factories, former radium processing sites, and NORM waste from oil and gas operations. Provides characterization, packaging, and licensed disposal of Ra-226 waste.
